faqs.question: Why do we feel tired and weak when we skip meals?
faqs.answer: When we skip meals, our body does not get the energy it needs to work. Food is like fuel for our body, just like petrol is fuel for a car. Without food, our body cannot run, think, or play properly. That is why we feel tired, weak, and sometimes even get a headache when we miss a meal.

faqs.question: What is the difference between hunger and appetite?
faqs.answer: Hunger is when your body really needs food and your stomach feels empty. Appetite is when you feel like eating something because it looks or smells good, even if you are not really hungry. For example, you might eat a gulab jamun after a full meal because of appetite, not hunger!

faqs.question: Why is dal and rice considered a good combination?
faqs.answer: Dal gives us proteins (body-building nutrients) and rice gives us carbohydrates (energy). When we eat them together, our body gets both energy and building blocks for growth. This is why dal-chawal or dal-roti is one of the most common and nutritious meals eaten across India.

faqs.question: Can we survive by eating only one type of food?
faqs.answer: No, we cannot stay healthy by eating only one type of food. Each food gives us different nutrients. If we eat only rice, we will get energy but our body will not grow properly and we will fall sick easily. We need foods from all groups - energy-giving, body-building, and protective foods.

faqs.question: Why do we need to eat green vegetables even though they don't taste as good as sweets?
faqs.answer: Green vegetables like spinach (palak), fenugreek (methi), and bottle gourd (lauki) are full of vitamins, minerals, and iron. They protect us from diseases, make our blood healthy, and help our eyes see better. Sweets give us only sugar and energy but no protection from illness.

faqs.question: Why should we wash our hands before eating?
faqs.answer: Our hands touch many things during the day - books, door handles, soil, and animals. Tiny germs that we cannot see stick to our hands. If we eat without washing hands, these germs go inside our body with the food and can make us sick with stomach ache, vomiting, or loose motions.

faqs.question: Is it okay to eat chips and cold drinks sometimes?
faqs.answer: Yes, eating junk food once in a while on special occasions is okay. But eating it every day is harmful because it has too much oil, salt, and sugar. It can lead to health problems like obesity, tooth decay, and weakness. Always try to eat more home-cooked food and keep junk food as an occasional treat.

faqs.question: Why do people in different parts of India eat different foods?
faqs.answer: People eat what grows easily in their region. In Kerala and Bengal, rice and fish are common because rice paddies and rivers are plentiful. In Punjab and Rajasthan, wheat-based rotis are common because wheat grows well there. The climate, soil, and water available in each area decide what crops grow best.

faqs.question: What is the mid-day meal scheme in schools?
faqs.answer: The mid-day meal is a free lunch given to students in government schools across India. It includes rice or roti, dal, vegetables, and sometimes eggs or fruits. This programme helps ensure that children get at least one nutritious meal every day, so they can study and learn better.

faqs.question: How can we make sure we are eating healthy every day?
faqs.answer: Here are some simple tips: (1) Eat a variety of foods from all food groups at every meal. (2) Include fruits and vegetables daily. (3) Drink plenty of clean water. (4) Avoid eating too many sweets, chips, and cold drinks. (5) Always wash your hands before eating. (6) Eat freshly cooked food and avoid stale food.
